expectations.text: Articles (a/an/the) are largely absent from prose
expectations.evidence: Articles dropped throughout: "Map/object grows forever, never trimmed", "Each request adds entry, nothing evicts", "Heap climbs linearly with traffic", "Closure captures large obj/buffer", "Outer fn returns", "Take snapshot", "Generate load", "Start with `--inspect` flag"

expectations.text: Response uses contractions (don't, can't, it's, etc.)
expectations.evidence: "GC can't collect", "don't ignore it", "Heap profiler won't catch it"

expectations.text: Response uses short synonyms or fragment patterns typical of caveman mode
expectations.evidence: "Outer fn returns" (fn for function), "Fastest way to find *where* memory allocated", "Growing `external` or `arrayBuffers` = native addon or `Buffer` leak", "Steady climb = leak. Plateau then GC = not leak"

expectations.text: Covers at least 2 common causes of Node.js memory leaks
expectations.evidence: Covers 8 causes: unbounded caches/global accumulation, event emitter listeners never removed, closures holding refs, timers/intervals not cleared, streams not consumed/destroyed, forgotten promise chains, module-level arrays as queues, native addons/external memory.

expectations.text: Mentions at least one concrete debugging tool or technique
expectations.evidence: Multiple tools mentioned: clinic.js (`npx clinic heapprofiler`), V8 inspector via `--inspect` and chrome://inspect, `--expose-gc` for manual GC isolation, `memwatch-next` package, `process.memoryUsage()` polling.
